title: Foundation cleanup and science-schema pass
summary: First structured handoff packet for Eco Explorer. This packet packages the current high-leverage foundation work so the main agent can simplify the shell, protect science accuracy, and preserve room for future biome growth without relying on chat history.

execution_order.goal: Shift the browser shell from landing page framing to a game-first screen.
execution_order.why_now: The current shell is the clearest mismatch with the product direction and it blocks any honest assessment of viewport and safe-area needs.

execution_order.goal: Fix inspectable metadata so organism and landmark content can stay science-accurate.
execution_order.why_now: The content schema should be corrected before more biome entries are added and before placeholder taxonomy spreads.

execution_order.goal: Re-evaluate viewport, aspect ratio, and safe-area recommendations after the shell cleanup lands.
execution_order.why_now: Viewport advice should be based on the simplified implementation rather than the current landing-page wrapper.

execution_order.goal: Review the refactor against focus, readability, and game-first feel.
execution_order.why_now: A grounded critique after implementation helps catch drift before the next content or biome pass.

guardrails: Do not change the internal render resolution just to make the outer shell feel fuller.
Keep onboarding and controls inside the in-game UI where possible instead of duplicating them in the page shell.
Preserve save behavior and deterministic hooks.
Treat science accuracy as a hard gate.
Avoid expanding src/engine/game.ts further unless the current chunk clearly benefits more from finishing than from extraction.
acceptance_focus: The browser page no longer leads with a large external title or controls card.
The game frame clearly dominates the viewport.
Landmark or habitat inspectables no longer require invented scientific names.
Tests or validations protect the new metadata rule.
Future agents can understand the current foundation sequence from this packet plus the queue.

followup_candidates.title: Extract overlay helpers from src/engine/game.ts if the next UI pass pushes more view code into that file.
followup_candidates.trigger: Promote only if the shell or schema work materially increases overlay complexity.

followup_candidates.title: Prepare a second-biome brief after the foundation pass is complete.
followup_candidates.trigger: Promote only after packet 001 work is done and the game-first shell direction is stable.

handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, and this packet. Execute the queue items in packet order unless queue status changes. Keep the game-first screen direction, science-accuracy guardrails, and deterministic behavior intact.
